success_awg2: AmneziaWG 2.0 config generated! Requires AmneziaVPN 4.8.12.9+ or a compatible AWG 2.0 client.
err_timeout: Request timed out. Please try again.
err_no_content: Config content is missing in the response.
err_html_response: Server returned HTML instead of JSON (no API). Run vercel dev or open the deployed site.
err_not_json: Response is not valid JSON. Check API availability.
info_message_p1: Config can be generated for any device. By default, all traffic goes through the tunnel. Route and DNS presets can be configured in the modal window via the ⚙ button next to the generate buttons.
info_message_p2: For Windows — two separate .bat files for each client version: 1.5 (Legacy) and 2.0; each references its own config file in the Downloads folder.
status_not_yet:
Monitoring data not yet collected.
Healthcheck runs every 30 min via GitHub Actions.
info_s1_p1: By default, all traffic goes through the tunnel (AllowedIPs = 0.0.0.0/0, ::/0). In settings (⚙ button) you can choose a DNS server and presets — only the required IPv4 subnets will be routed through the tunnel. IPv6 can be enabled optionally, but it increases the route count 2–3× and may cause issues on routers and mobile clients. Safe limit — 1000 IPv4 CIDR.
info_s1_p2: IP ranges can be replaced manually. Up-to-date lists at opencck.org and rockblack.pro.
info_legacy_li2: Masks only the start of the connection
info_legacy_li3: Works with Cloudflare WARP without a server
info_awg2_li1: Dynamic headers + random padding in all packets
info_awg2_li2: Mimics QUIC/DNS throughout the entire session
info_awg2_li3: Harder to detect with heuristic DPI analysis
info_s3_title: ⚠ Is a server required for AWG 2.0?
info_s3_p1: For Cloudflare WARP — no. Configs from this generator work with Cloudflare's public server without any server-side configuration.
info_s3_p2: For your own server — yes. Self-hosted AWG 2.0 requires an updated kernel/module amneziawg-linux-kernel-module on the server and new client configs (old AWG 1.5 configs become incompatible). See the official documentation for details.
instruction_step1:
Download and install AmneziaWG:
Follow the download link and get the installer for Windows. Install to the default path "C:\Program Files\...".
instruction_step2:
Generate the config file:
After installation, click "Generate AWG 1.5" (Legacy, file AmneziaWarp.conf) or "Generate AWG 2.0" (file AmneziaWarp-AWG2.conf) — depending on your client version. Save the config to the Downloads folder: the scheduler bat expects exactly these file names.
instruction_step3:
Download and run the scheduler bat:
Download "Scheduler for 1.5" or "Scheduler for 2.0" — the one matching your config. Run the chosen .bat as administrator once: it creates a Windows Task Scheduler task that runs at login. When switching client versions, remove the old task in Task Scheduler or use a different .bat (task names inside the files differ).
instruction_conclusion: After that, AmneziaWG will automatically connect to the config from Downloads at login 🥸 Adjust the path to amneziawg.exe in the bat if needed for your installation.
thank_you_text: Guys, and especially network engineers, I'm really glad my little tool is getting some use. I often don't know if it still works; the world changes too fast. Always happy to make friends — come visit: Telegram channel.
routes_hint: Check presets — their IPv4 subnets will be added to AllowedIPs. If nothing is selected, all traffic goes through the tunnel (0.0.0.0/0, ::/0). The limit of 1000 IPv4 CIDR protects routers and mobile devices from routing table overflow.
ipv6_toggle_title: IPv6 routes increase the number of entries 2–3×. Enable only if your client supports IPv6 and your router can handle a large number of routes.
ignore_limit_title: Removes the preset selection block when the limit is exceeded. Use at your own risk — too many routes may cause issues on routers and mobile clients.
preset_none_selected: No presets selected — all traffic goes through the tunnel.
preset_offline_warning: Presets loaded from local list. CIDR estimate and config generation with AllowedIPs by presets require the API — run vercel dev or open the deployed site.
preset_over_limit_msg: Some devices may become unstable. Consider disabling some categories.
scheduler_fail: Failed to download bat. The site must be served over HTTP (not file://) — or copy files from the public/static folder in the repository.
router_mode_title: Reduces junk-train parameters (Jc ≤ 2, Jmin/Jmax ≤ 128 bytes) for compatibility with MikroTik, GL.iNet, Keenetic and OpenWrt routers with limited memory.
